sprint.name: Sanders Intelligence — In-progress bucket (2026-05-21)
sprint.source: Asana project 'Sanders intelligence app', section 'In progress' (re-pulled 2026-05-21)
sprint.summary: Three feature tasks, all data/metrics work on the Executive Summary 'Top Risk Supplier' card and the Vendor View page. Tasks A and B both restructure the SAME card and must be implemented together as one coherent layout change. Task C adds profit/COGS metrics to Vendor View using the existing sku_profit_metrics data pipeline.
stack_reminders: React 18 + TypeScript + Vite; Tailwind design tokens (text1/text2/surface/surface2/accent/success/warning/danger)
TanStack Query v5; existing query keys ['inventory','latest'], ['inventory','trends'], ['sku_metrics']
Supabase Postgres + RLS; all multi-row fetches must paginate past the 1000-row cap with a .range() loop
Recharts for charts; lucide-react for icons — do not add new libraries
Currency/number formatting: always use fmtCurrency / fmtNumber from @/lib/utils
Pure data transforms belong in *.helpers.ts files next to the page, with unit tests in app/src/__tests__/ (this pattern already exists for ActionCenter, ExecutiveSummary, VendorView, TaskModal)

inventory_records_relevant_fields.product_code: string — primary SKU key; equals sku_profit_metrics.planning_sku and sku_price_metrics.planning_sku
inventory_records_relevant_fields.supplier_description: string — the vendor name used for grouping throughout the app
inventory_records_relevant_fields.status: one of: Ok | Excess stock | Surplus orders | Potential s/o | Stocked out | New item
inventory_records_relevant_fields.on_hand_value: number — $ value of on-hand stock at cost
inventory_records_relevant_fields.cost_price: number — per-unit cost (NOTE: some rows are 0 — guard divisions)
inventory_records_relevant_fields.selling_price: number — per-unit selling price (NOTE: some rows are 0/empty — guard divisions)
inventory_records_relevant_fields.unsatisfied_customer_orders_units: number — backorder units; a SKU is 'backordered' when this is > 0
inventory_records_relevant_fields.unsatisfied_customer_orders_value: number — $ value of unfilled customer orders

status_buckets.Backordered: unsatisfied_customer_orders_units > 0 — NOTE: this is orthogonal to status; a backordered SKU is ALSO counted in one of the status buckets above. The four percentages therefore do NOT sum to 100%.

sku_profit_metrics.table: public.sku_profit_metrics — fetched by useSkuMetrics() hook in app/src/hooks/useSkuMetrics.ts
sku_profit_metrics.windows_available: today, 7d, 30d — there is NO 60d or 90d window in this table
sku_profit_metrics.graceful_absence: useSkuMetrics returns empty maps if the table is missing (Postgres error 42P01) — UI must degrade to '—'.

sku_price_metrics.fields: selling_price, price_min, price_max, price_avg, price_source, price_source_count — a single current price row per SKU, NOT per-window

shared_conventions.vendor_grouping: Group inventory_records by supplier_description (fallback 'Unknown supplier'), exactly as buildTopRiskSuppliers and VendorView already do.
shared_conventions.sku_metric_join: For a record r, its profit metric is skuMetrics.profitBySku.get(r.product_code); price metric is skuMetrics.priceBySku.get(r.product_code). Both may be undefined.
shared_conventions.division_guards: Never divide by zero: percent/ratio is null when the denominator is 0; render null as an em dash '—'.
shared_conventions.helpers_and_tests: Add new pure functions to ExecutiveSummary.helpers.ts / VendorView.helpers.ts and extend the matching __tests__ files.

tasks.title: Executive Summary — restructure the Top Risk Supplier card into a status breakdown (% + $)
tasks.asana_note_verbatim: We have action items by suppliers. We have to show percent of SKUs are at risk, percent of backordered etc, percent of ok, $ amounts for same statuses.
tasks.depends_on: Implement together with ASANA-1214971312304123 (Task B) — both restructure the same card row; design the layout once.

current_state.today: The 'Top Risk Supplier — Requires Attention' section renders one card per vendor (top 10). Each card row currently shows 6 metric cells: '# at-risk SKUs', '$ at risk', 'Lowest Days OH', 'Rec Order $', 'Open Backorders $', 'Fill rate'. Cards are sorted by recOrderValue desc. The TopRiskSupplier interface in the helper defines: supplier, atRiskSkuCount, atRiskOnHandValue, minDaysOnHand, recOrderValue, openBackorderValue, fillRate.

tasks.decision: REPLACE the current 6 columns entirely with a per-status breakdown (chosen by product owner).

required_outcome.per_vendor_metrics: For each vendor card, compute and display these four status groups, each as BOTH a percentage of the vendor's SKU count AND a dollar amount:

groups.label: Backordered
groups.dollars: sum(unsatisfied_customer_orders_value) over backordered SKUs — NOTE: backordered uses BACKORDER $ (value of unfilled orders), not on_hand_value, because that is the meaningful dollar figure for unfilled demand. The other three groups use on_hand_value.

required_outcome.display_format: Each group shows on two lines: the percentage prominent (e.g. '32%'), the dollar amount secondary beneath it (e.g. '$46,378'). Label above in text-xs text2. Use the group color for the percentage value.
required_outcome.sublines: Keep a vendor name + a one-line context subline. Update the subline to read e.g. '<atRiskPct>% at risk · <okPct>% OK' so the card is scannable when collapsed.
required_outcome.card_sort_order: Sort vendors by At Risk dollars (sum of on_hand_value over At Risk SKUs) descending. Still slice to top 10. (recOrderValue is no longer displayed; it may be dropped from the interface or kept internally — implementer's choice, but the displayed ranking must be by At Risk $.)
required_outcome.empty_state: Preserve the existing 'No at-risk suppliers — inventory health is good' empty state. A vendor with zero At Risk SKUs should not appear (keep the existing .filter(row => atRiskSkuCount > 0)).

files_to_modify: app/src/pages/csuite/ExecutiveSummary.helpers.ts — extend the TopRiskSupplier interface and buildTopRiskSuppliers() to compute okPct/okValue, atRiskPct/atRiskValue, excessPct/excessValue, backorderedPct/backorderedValue, plus totalSkuCount
app/src/pages/csuite/ExecutiveSummary.tsx — rewrite the Top Risk Supplier card row JSX
app/src/__tests__/ExecutiveSummary.helpers.test.ts — extend tests for the new fields
edge_cases: Vendor with totalVendorSkuCount === 0 cannot occur (a vendor only exists because it has records) but guard the percent division anyway.
A SKU counted in both At Risk and Backordered is correct and expected — do not de-duplicate; the percentages are independent and need not sum to 100%.
Dollar values of 0 render as '$0' via fmtCurrency, not '—'.
acceptance_criteria: Each of the top-10 vendor cards shows four labelled groups — OK, At Risk, Excess, Backordered — each with a percentage and a dollar amount.
Percentages are computed against the vendor's TOTAL SKU count, so At Risk% + OK% + Excess% can be less than or greater-than-overlapping 100% (Backordered overlaps intentionally).
At Risk and OK dollar figures equal the sum of on_hand_value over those SKUs; Backordered dollar equals sum of unsatisfied_customer_orders_value.
Cards are ordered by At Risk $ descending.
The previous 6 columns (# at-risk SKUs, Lowest Days OH, Rec Order $, Open Backorders $, Fill rate as standalone cells) are gone.
Clicking a card still navigates to /purchasing/inventory?vendor=<encoded vendor> (preserve existing behavior).
Helper unit tests cover the four percentages and four dollar sums with a small fixture.

tasks.title: Executive Summary — add Average Profit, Average Selling Price, and Margin to the Top Risk Supplier card
tasks.asana_note_verbatim: (no description provided in Asana — title only: 'Add Average Profit, Average Selling price into the Executive summary Top Risk supplier section, Margin')
tasks.depends_on: Implement together with ASANA-1214748761687759 (Task A) — same card.
tasks.decision: Use TODAY'S inventory snapshot, averaged across ALL of the vendor's SKUs (chosen by product owner). Do NOT use the sku_profit_metrics 7d/30d accrual data for this task — use inventory_records.selling_price and inventory_records.cost_price directly.

new_metrics_per_vendor.label: Avg Selling Price
new_metrics_per_vendor.formula: mean(selling_price) over the vendor's SKUs where selling_price > 0

new_metrics_per_vendor.label: Avg Profit
new_metrics_per_vendor.formula: mean(selling_price - cost_price) over the same SKU set (selling_price > 0). Equivalent to avgSellingPrice - avgCostPrice over that set.
new_metrics_per_vendor.note: Per-unit profit. Color the value danger (red) if negative.

new_metrics_per_vendor.label: Margin
new_metrics_per_vendor.note: Color danger if margin < 0, warning if 0–15%, default otherwise.

required_outcome.sku_set_definition: ALL SKUs the vendor supplies (every record grouped under that supplier_description), EXCLUDING records with selling_price <= 0 or null from the three averages (those rows have no usable price and would corrupt the mean / cause divide-by-zero in margin).
required_outcome.no_valid_skus: If a vendor has zero SKUs with selling_price > 0, render all three metrics as '—'.
required_outcome.placement: Add these three metrics into the same Top Risk Supplier card designed in Task A. Recommended layout: a two-tier metric area per card — tier 1 = the four status groups from Task A, tier 2 = Avg Selling Price, Avg Profit, Margin. Use a subtle divider or spacing between tiers. Keep the card height reasonable; the existing card uses a horizontal flex with the vendor name on the left and a metric grid on the right.

files_to_modify: app/src/pages/csuite/ExecutiveSummary.helpers.ts — extend TopRiskSupplier + buildTopRiskSuppliers() with avgSellingPrice, avgProfit, marginPct (number | null)
app/src/pages/csuite/ExecutiveSummary.tsx — render the three new metrics in tier 2 of the card
app/src/__tests__/ExecutiveSummary.helpers.test.ts — add tests, including a vendor where some SKUs have selling_price 0 (must be excluded) and a vendor with no priced SKUs (→ null)
edge_cases: selling_price > 0 but cost_price === 0 → profit = selling_price, margin = 100%. This is allowed (treat as valid).
Negative margin (cost_price > selling_price) is allowed and must be shown in red, not hidden.
Mixing currencies / units is not a concern — all figures are already in the same currency.
acceptance_criteria: Each top-10 vendor card shows Avg Selling Price, Avg Profit, and Margin in addition to the Task A status breakdown.
The three averages exclude SKUs with selling_price <= 0.
Margin equals (avgSellingPrice - avgCostPrice) / avgSellingPrice * 100 and is consistent with the displayed Avg Selling Price and Avg Profit.
A vendor with no priced SKUs shows '—' for all three.
Negative Avg Profit / Margin renders in the danger color.
Helper unit tests cover the exclusion rule and the null case.

tasks.title: Vendor View — add COGS % of income and Avg Selling Price per time window
tasks.asana_note_verbatim: (no description in Asana — title only: 'Feature on vendor view after SI app data cogs of good % of income & AVG Selling price 30 60 90 day')

scope_decision.windows: The Asana title says '30 / 60 / 90 day'. The existing sku_profit_metrics table only carries today / 7d / 30d windows — there is NO 60d or 90d data. Per product-owner decision, BUILD WITH today / 7d / 30d (the windows that exist). Do not block on backend work. If 60d/90d are wanted later, they require new columns on sku_profit_metrics first — out of scope here.
scope_decision.placement: BOTH a summary column on the main vendor table AND a per-window breakdown inside the expanded vendor panel (chosen by product owner).

current_state.today: VendorView builds VendorRow objects (one per vendor) via a useMemo over useInventoryAnalysis records. The main table has sortable columns: Vendor, Supplier Code, Total SKUs, OK, At Risk, Excess, New Items, Backorder Units, Rec. Order Qty, Rec. Order Value, On-Hand Value. Clicking a row expands a per-vendor SKU sub-table. VendorView does NOT currently use useSkuMetrics().
current_state.data_hook: app/src/hooks/useSkuMetrics.ts already exists and is used by InventoryBrowser and InboundPipeline. It returns { profitBySku: Map<planning_sku, SkuProfitMetric>, priceBySku: Map<planning_sku, SkuPriceMetric> }.

definitions.per_vendor_window_aggregates: For each vendor and each window w, sum across that vendor's SKUs (look up profitBySku.get(product_code); treat a missing metric as all-zeros): revenue_w = Σ revenue_<w>; profit_w = Σ accrual_profit_<w>; units_w = Σ units_<w>.
definitions.cogs_pct_of_income: COGS_w = revenue_w - profit_w (cost of goods sold). COGS % of income_w = revenue_w > 0 ? (COGS_w / revenue_w) * 100 : null. Interpretation: of every dollar of revenue, what share went to cost of goods. Lower is better.
definitions.avg_selling_price: avgSellingPrice_w = units_w > 0 ? revenue_w / units_w : null. This is the realized average price per unit sold over the window (revenue divided by units sold).
definitions.use_accrual_not_cash: Use accrual_profit_<w> (not cash_profit_<w>) for the COGS calculation.

main_table_column.new_column: Add ONE new sortable column to the main vendor table: 'COGS %' showing the 30-day COGS % of income (cogsPct30d). Render as a one-decimal percent; '—' when null (no metric data / zero revenue). Place it after 'On-Hand Value'. It must participate in the existing SortableTh sort mechanism (numeric sort; nulls sort last in both directions).
main_table_column.optional_color: Optionally color the value: warning when COGS % > 80, default otherwise — implementer discretion.

expanded_panel_breakdown.what: Inside the expanded vendor panel (the block that appears when a vendor row is clicked), add a compact metrics strip ABOVE the existing per-vendor SKU sub-table.
expanded_panel_breakdown.content: A small 3-column-by-2-row grid (or equivalent) showing, for each window today / 7d / 30d: 'COGS %' and 'Avg Selling Price'. Label each window clearly ('Today', '7-day', '30-day').
expanded_panel_breakdown.empty_state: If the vendor has no SKUs with profit metrics, show a single muted line: 'No profit metrics available for this vendor.'

data_wiring: Call useSkuMetrics() inside VendorView (alongside the existing useInventoryAnalysis()).
Extend the VendorRow build useMemo to compute the per-window aggregates and the derived cogsPct / avgSellingPrice for today/7d/30d. Add skuMetrics to the useMemo dependency array.
Respect the existing category filter — when a category is selected, the COGS/price aggregates must be computed over the filtered record subset, consistent with all other VendorRow fields.
While useSkuMetrics is loading, the COGS column may show '—'; do not block the whole page on it (the inventory data is the primary content).

files_to_modify: app/src/pages/purchasing/VendorView.tsx — import & call useSkuMetrics; extend VendorRow type + the vendorRows useMemo; add the 'COGS %' SortableTh + cell; add the expanded-panel metrics strip
app/src/pages/purchasing/VendorView.helpers.ts — add a pure helper, e.g. buildVendorWindowMetrics(records, profitBySku) returning { cogsPct, avgSellingPrice } per window; keep it pure and testable
app/src/__tests__/VendorView.helpers.test.ts — unit tests for the new helper (including missing-metric SKUs, zero-revenue, zero-units cases)
edge_cases: A SKU with no row in sku_profit_metrics contributes 0 to every sum — it must not break the aggregate.
sku_profit_metrics table absent entirely (useSkuMetrics returns empty maps) → every vendor shows '—' for COGS %, and the expanded panel shows the 'No profit metrics available' message. No crash.
Negative profit (loss) → COGS % > 100 is mathematically possible; display it as-is (do not clamp), it is a meaningful signal.
acceptance_criteria: The main vendor table has a new sortable 'COGS %' column showing the 30-day COGS % of income; sorting by it works and nulls sort last.
Expanding a vendor shows a metrics strip with COGS % and Avg Selling Price for Today, 7-day, and 30-day windows.
COGS % equals (revenue - accrual_profit) / revenue * 100 aggregated across the vendor's SKUs for that window.
Avg Selling Price equals revenue / units sold for that window.
Selecting a category filter recomputes the COGS/price metrics over the filtered SKUs.
When sku_profit_metrics has no data for a vendor, the UI shows '—' / the empty message and does not error.
The new helper has unit tests covering missing metrics, zero revenue, and zero units.
No regression to existing Vendor View columns, sorting, expansion, category filter, or the expanded SKU sub-table filters.
explicitly_out_of_scope: 60-day and 90-day windows — not supported by current data; would require new sku_profit_metrics columns and pipeline work.
Adding COGS/price data to the CSV export.
Per-SKU COGS rows inside the expanded sub-table (this task is vendor-level aggregates only).

global_acceptance: App builds and runs: `cd app && npm run dev` and `npm run build` with no new TypeScript or ESLint errors.
No new console errors or warnings on first paint of /executive and /purchasing/vendors.
All new pure helpers have unit tests in app/src/__tests__/ and the existing test suite still passes.
Supabase fetches (useSkuMetrics) continue to paginate correctly and degrade gracefully when a table is missing.
Tasks A and B are delivered together as one coherent Top Risk Supplier card redesign; Task C is a separate, independent change to Vendor View.
Each Asana task can be moved to Done after its acceptance criteria are manually verified in the running app.
verification_plan: Task A: open /executive, scroll to 'Top Risk Supplier'; confirm each card shows OK / At Risk / Excess / Backordered as % + $; cross-check one vendor's At Risk % against Vendor View's At Risk count ÷ Total SKUs.
Task B: on the same cards confirm Avg Selling Price, Avg Profit, Margin appear; pick a vendor and hand-verify Margin = (avgSell - avgCost)/avgSell; confirm a vendor with unpriced SKUs shows '—'.

Task C: open /purchasing/vendors, confirm the 'COGS %' column sorts; expand a vendor and confirm the Today/7d/30d COGS % + Avg Selling Price strip; apply a category filter and confirm the metrics update; temporarily verify graceful '—' behavior if sku_profit_metrics is empty.
Regression: confirm Executive Summary's other sections (health bar, KPI strip, charts, weekly stacked bar) and Vendor View's existing columns/expansion/filters are unaffected.
notes_unrelated_to_this_sprint: Separate from these 3 tasks, prior QA found open bugs not in the In-progress bucket: (1) the 'Inventory Value Distribution' pie chart on Executive Summary renders no segments, (2) the sidebar collapse toggle does not animate width on expand→collapse, (3) a dev-only false-positive console warning in ActionCenter. These are tracked separately and are out of scope for this prompt.
